step.text: Login to MCA portal with DSC
step.name: Step 1

step.text: Select DIR-3 KYC or DIR-3 KYC-WEB
step.name: Step 2

mainEntity.name: Is DIN eKYC mandatory every year?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Every DIN holder must file DIR-3 KYC by September 30. Failure: DIN deactivated. Reactivation requires \u20b95,000 fee + fresh KYC filing.
PAN, Aadhaar, personal mobile number and email (for OTP verification), residential address proof, and DSC.

mainEntity.name: What is DIR-3 KYC and who must file it?

acceptedAnswer.text: Annual KYC update mandatory for every person holding an active DIN. Due by September 30 every year. DIR-3 KYC (with documents) for first filing. DIR-3 KYC-WEB (OTP only) for subsequent years with no changes.

mainEntity.name: What happens if DIR-3 KYC is not filed?

acceptedAnswer.text: DIN gets deactivated. Cannot sign any MCA forms, cannot file ROC documents. Penalty: ₹5,000 for delayed filing. Reactivation requires filing DIR-3 KYC with payment.

mainEntity.name: What documents are needed for DIR-3 KYC?

acceptedAnswer.text: PAN (mandatory), Aadhaar, passport-size photo, mobile number, personal email, permanent address proof, and present address proof. DSC required for filing.

mainEntity.name: Can NRI directors file DIR-3 KYC?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. NRI directors must file using their Indian PAN and foreign address proof. If no Indian mobile: use foreign mobile number. Video KYC may be required for first-time foreign address.

mainEntity.name: Is DIR-3 KYC different from DIN application?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. DIN application (DIR-3) is one-time for obtaining DIN. DIR-3 KYC is annual compliance for maintaining active DIN. Both are necessary — DIN first, then annual KYC.
